senses.definition: able to be capitalized or made into a capital letter or to be financed with capital.
examples: The word at the start of the sentence is capitalizable.
This term is capitalizable in certain financial reports.

senses.definition: the act of using capital letters or the process of providing capital (finance) to a business.
examples: In English, capitalization refers to capital letters at the beginning of sentences.
The company underwent capitalization to fund expansion.

senses.definition: plural form of capitalization; instances of capital letters or the act of providing capital.
examples: The document had several unusual capitalizations.
These capitalizations reflect proper nouns.

senses.definition: to write or print a letter as a capital letter; to use capital letters at the beginning of sentences or for proper nouns.
examples: Capitalize the first word of the sentence.
Capitalize proper nouns like London and July.

senses.definition: to provide capital (finance) for a business or project.
examples: The investors agreed to capitalize the startup.
They capitalized the project with new funding.

senses.definition: written with capital letters at the beginning of words or names.
examples: The capitalized word is at the start of the sentence.
Capitalized proper nouns must be used.

senses.definition: a person or tool that capitalizes text or provides capital funding.
examples: The editor acted as a capitalizer of the manuscript.
An investor can be seen as a capitalizer of startups.

senses.definition: plural of capitalizer; people or tools that capitalizes.
examples: Several capitalizers were involved in the funding round.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of capitalize; to write in capital letters or to take advantage of a situation for gain.
examples: The headline capitalizes the first letter of each word.
The company capitalizes on market trends.

senses.definition: present participle of capitalize; the act of writing in capital letters or leveraging something for gain.
examples: Capitalizing the proper nouns helps readability.
They are capitalizing on a weak competitor.

senses.definition: in a capital manner; used in some legal or historical contexts to mean 'in a capital manner' or 'in a capital city'.
examples: The city was capitally located for trade.
Capitally, the fortress stood high above the river.

senses.definition: the quality of being capital or proper; not a common usage in contemporary English.
examples: The essay discusses capitalness in typography.

senses.definition: the expensive money or assets used to fund something; also the capital letters used in writing.
examples: The company raised new capitals.
Capitals are the letters that begin each proper noun.

senses.definition: Spanish for captain; title used in sports or maritime contexts.
examples: El capitan del barco es muy experimentado.
Capitan del equipo lideró la victoria.

senses.definition: Spanish feminine form of captain; female captain.
examples: La capitana dio la orden.
Capitana lideró la misión con valentía.

senses.definition: plural of capitano in Italian; captain.
examples: I capitani della squadra hanno parlato dopo la partita.

senses.definition: Italian masculine form of captain; a rank or title.
examples: Il capitano della nave diede l'ordine.

senses.definition: Spanish plural of capitán; captains.
examples: Los capitanos lideraron la expedición.

senses.definition: plural of captain in English; multiple captains.
examples: The capitans coordinated the teams' efforts.

senses.definition: to reveal or proclaim; not a common English verb; appears in romance languages as root forms.
examples: Capitare is not standard English; see notes on usage.

senses.definition: second-person singular past indicative in Romance languages; not standard English.
examples: Capitasti è una forma italiana.

senses.definition: to form or cause to form a head or head-like structure; to strike a blow with the head in sports; in anatomy, to form a capitate bone connection.
examples: The drive will capitate the team with a strong win.
Capitate bones connect to the wrist.

senses.definition: having a head or head-like feature; used in anatomy to describe joints relating to the capitate bone.
examples: Capitated joints were examined by the doctor.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of capitate; to form into or connect with a head-like structure.
examples: It capitates the bone within the joint.

senses.definition: likely a misspelling or rare form not standard in English; no dictionary sense.
a payment system where a fixed amount of money is paid per person; a head tax.
examples: Capitation is used to fund healthcare services per patient.
The capitation rate is negotiated yearly.
